output_summary: profiled datasets: dbpedia, metacritic, sales.
This run was an initial draft of the pipeline.

The pipeline was constructed to load XML datasets, normalize key attributes, perform LLM-based schema matching to align schemas to dbpedia, and then apply standard blocking on normalized name, platform, and release year fields.
Rule-based matching was configured using string comparators with Jaccard similarity and numeric comparators with a max difference of 1, weighted primarily on name_norm.

Data fusion was set up with a strategy that applies the longest_string fuser to most string attributes and maximum fuser to numeric attributes like criticScore, userScore, globalSales, and release_year_num.
Singleton records were excluded from the fusion output.
The fusion strategy uses a dominant longest_string fuser for most attributes to preserve the most complete string values, while numeric attributes use a maximum fuser to retain the highest values.
Matching behavior is rule-based with weighted comparators emphasizing name similarity, platform, release year, and developer fields.
Normalization functions were added for name, platform, developer, publisher, and release year to create consistent blocking and matching keys.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.
This run was an initial draft of the evaluation adaption.
The dominant evaluation strategy used in this run is a rule-based fusion strategy with registered evaluation functions for various game attributes.

Evaluation functions were grouped by field families including core game attributes (name, developer, publisher, platform, ESRB) using tokenized matching, releaseYear using year-only matching, numeric fields (criticScore, userScore, globalSales) using numeric tolerance matching, and nested/list attributes (genres_genre) using set equality matching.
Helper functions were added to align fused cluster rows to gold IDs for evaluation, including parsing source IDs and detecting gold ID prefixes.
A function was added to build an evaluation view that aligns fused data with gold data for evaluation purposes.
Support for loading XML gold data and reading fused CSV data was included.
Evaluation functions were registered to the fusion strategy with a compact summary printed.
A DataFusionEvaluator was instantiated with debug options and used to evaluate the fused data against the gold data.
Evaluation results were printed and saved to a JSON file.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 80.67%; evaluation attempt counter is 1.
This run was an evaluation refinement of the existing pipeline.

The data fusion strategy was changed to use 'prefer_higher_trust' fusers for most attributes including developer, publisher, ESRB, criticScore, userScore, and globalSales instead of previously used 'longest_string' or 'maximum' fusers.
The fusion for attributes name, releaseYear, platform, series, and their normalized variants continued to use 'longest_string'.
Numeric normalized fields such as release_year_num, critic_score_num, user_score_num, and global_sales_num use the 'maximum' fuser.
The matching weights were adjusted to increase the weight of the 'name_norm' comparator and the threshold was raised from 0.75 to 0.78.
Blocking was simplified to use only 'name_norm' as the blocking key instead of multiple keys including platform_norm and release_year_num.
Singleton retention was disabled in the fusion engine.
The fusion behavior shifted from relying on longest string or maximum value selection to preferring values from higher-trust sources for most attributes.
Matching behavior was strengthened by normalizing developer fields and increasing the weight on name similarity while keeping platform and release year as disambiguators.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.
This run was an evaluation refinement.
The dominant evaluation strategy used was a rule-based fusion strategy with tokenized matching, year-only matching, set equality matching, and numeric tolerance matching.
Core game attributes such as name, developer, publisher, platform, and ESRB rating were evaluated using tokenized_match.
The releaseYear field was evaluated using year_only_match to compare only the year portion.
Numeric or numeric-like fields such as criticScore, userScore, and globalSales were evaluated using numeric_tolerance_match to allow small differences.
Nested or list-like fields such as genres_genre were evaluated using set_equality_match.
Added helper functions to align fused cluster rows to gold IDs for evaluation.
Implemented list normalization utilities to detect and normalize list-like columns before evaluation.
Enhanced ID alignment coverage diagnostics to compare direct cluster IDs and mapped evaluation IDs against gold IDs.
Updated evaluation function registration to include all relevant fields and default to set_equality_match for any detected list-like columns not explicitly registered.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 67.23%; previous logged accuracy was 80.67%; evaluation attempt counter is 2.
This run was a refinement of the existing pipeline based on evaluation results.
The data fusion strategy was adjusted to prefer higher-trust sources for most attributes instead of using longest string or maximum value.
Specifically, platform, platform_norm, and releaseYear fusion changed from longest_string to prefer_higher_trust.
Critic_score_num and user_score_num fusion changed from maximum to prefer_higher_trust.
New fusion attributes userScore_str, name_loose, and criticScore_str were added with fusers prefer_higher_trust and longest_string respectively.
Singleton retention was disabled in this iteration.
The fusion behavior shifted towards relying more on source trustworthiness rather than string length or maximum numeric values.
This means that for most attributes, values from more trusted sources like metacritic or sales are preferred over simply taking the longest string or highest numeric value.
The blocking strategy was also updated to use a looser canonical name (name_loose) to improve recall across editions and variants.
Normalization dictionaries for developer/publisher and platform synonyms were expanded and refined.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.
This run was an evaluation refinement.
The dominant evaluation strategy used was rule-based fusion strategy with registered evaluation functions for each attribute.
Core entity attributes such as name, developer, publisher, platform, and ESRB rating were evaluated using tokenized_match.
Date fields like releaseYear were evaluated using year_only_match.
Numeric or numeric-like fields such as criticScore, userScore, and globalSales were evaluated using numeric_tolerance_match.
Nested or list-like fields such as genres_genre and any other detected list-like columns were evaluated using set_equality_match.
Added optional normalization of list-like columns to handle differences in CSV and XML representations.
Improved ID alignment by mapping fused clusters to gold IDs using _fusion_sources and selecting the highest-confidence fused row per eval_id.
Refined the import and module loading logic for list normalization utilities to ensure robustness.
Updated evaluation function registration to include all detected list-like columns with set equality matching.
Enhanced debug output and structured evaluation metrics printing.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 84.87%; previous logged accuracy was 67.23%; evaluation attempt counter is 3.
